title: Dead Code, Unused Dependencies, and Reachability · Failure Case: Safe Removal
summary: Remove unreachable code and dependencies with evidence rather than guesswork. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates safe removal increment in a dead-code and dependency audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.
Account for plugins, reflection, generated entry points, and framework conventions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Predict Safe Removal
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates dead code. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates dead code. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Safe Removal contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.

content.text: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates dead code. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Safe Removal, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a dead-code and dependency audit.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Deleting unused-looking code is always a harmless cleanup. This fails the Safe Removal contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Clang AddressSanitizer and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (Clang 23 development documentation) — Controls native-code runtime sanitizer purpose, build profiles, output, limitations, and security cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.

steps.title: Build Safe Removal evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-safe-removal-wivmgp: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Safe Removal into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-safe-removal-wivmgp:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Safe Removal model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Safe Removal applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.
